metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4960
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Authors generated transgenic mice that express a constitutively active form of Gαi2 (GNAI2) to test which of the effects of mGluR2 (GRM2) on mossy fiber synaptic plasticity can be accounted for by signaling mechanisms involving inhibitory Gα subunits.

Figure 2a-c: Constitutively active Gαi2 expression does not alter synaptic input-output relations or paired-pulse facilitation at mossy fiber-CA3 synapses.

Constitutively active Gαi2 in mossy fiber-CA3 synapses;
- occludes transient suppression of synaptic transmission by mGluR2 Activation (Fig. 2c-d)
- enhances LTD (Fig. 3a)
- rescues the LTD deficit caused by mGluR2 blockade (Fig. 3b)
- inhibits induction of LTP (Fig. 3c)

Authors summarised these results in the abstract as follows;
"mGluR2 participates in at least three types of mossy fiber synaptic plasticity, (i) transient suppression of synaptic transmission, (ii) long-term depression (LTD), and (iii) inhibition of long-term potentiation (LTP), and we find that inhibitory Gα signaling is sufficient to account for the actions of mGluR2 in each. The fact that constitutively active Gαi2 occludes the transient suppression of synaptic transmission by mGluR2, while enhancing LTD, suggests further that these two forms of plasticity are expressed via different mechanisms.". Experimental description: Result section: "We generated transgenic mice that express a constitutively active form of Gαi2 to test ..."
